Description: In Svelte 5 use $state rune for reactive declarations
Code Good:
<script>
  let count = $state(0)
</script>
<button onclick={() => count++}>{count}</button>
Code Bad:
<script>
  let count = 0 // Not reactive in Svelte 5
</script>

Description: Derived state automatically updates when dependencies change
Do: Use $derived for values computed from state

Description: Effects run after state changes for side effects like logging or DOM manipulation
Do: Use $effect for reactions to state changes

Description: Effects should only be used for side effects not for state derivation
Code Good:
// Correct
let total = $derived(items.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0))

Description: Use raw state for objects that should not be deeply reactive
Do: Use $state.raw for performance with large objects
Code Good:
let largeData = $state.raw(fetchedData)
// Only reassignment triggers updates

Description: Use $props to declare component props in Svelte 5
Don't: Use export let for props in Svelte 5
Code Good: {name} is {age}
Code Bad:
<script>
  export let name
  export let age = 18
</script>
// Svelte 4 syntax

Guideline: Provide default values for optional props
Description: Always provide defaults for optional props to avoid undefined
Code Bad:
let { title, size, disabled } = $props()
// All potentially undefined

Description: Capture and spread remaining props to child elements
Do: Use rest syntax for prop forwarding
Code Good:
let { class: className, ...rest } = $props()
<button class={className} {...rest}>
  <slot />
</button>
Code Bad:
let { class: className, onclick, disabled, type } = $props()
<button class={className} {onclick} {disabled} {type}>

Description: Define prop types for better IDE support and safety
Code Good:
<script lang="ts">
  interface Props {
    name: string
    age?: number
  }
  let { name, age = 18 }: Props = $props()
</script>
Code Bad:
<script lang="ts">
  let { name, age } = $props() // any types
</script>

Description: Bind directive creates two-way data flow for form elements
Code Good:
<input bind:value={name} />
<input bind:checked={agreed} type="checkbox" />
<select bind:value={selected}>
Code Bad:
<input
  value={name}
  oninput={(e) => name = e.target.value}
/>

Description: Use bind: to create two-way binding with component props
Do: Use bind: when parent needs to read child state
Code Good:
<Slider bind:value={volume} />
// Parent can read and write volume
Code Bad:
<Slider
  value={volume}
  onchange={(v) => volume = v}
/>
// More verbose

Description: Access DOM elements directly when needed
Do: Use bind:this for DOM element references
Code Good:
<script>
  let canvas = $state()
  $effect(() => {
    const ctx = canvas?.getContext('2d')
  })
</script>
<canvas bind:this={canvas}></canvas>
Code Bad:
<script>
  let canvas
  onMount(() => {
    canvas = document.querySelector('canvas')
  })
</script>

Description: Bindings can make data flow harder to trace
Standard DOM events use the on: directive pattern
Do: Use onclick onsubmit etc for event handlers
Code Good:
<button onclick={handleClick}>Click</button>
<form onsubmit={handleSubmit}>
Code Bad:
<button use:click={handleClick}>
// Wrong directive

Description: Use event modifiers or manual event methods
Do: Call event methods in handler or use modifiers
Don't: Forget to prevent default on forms
Code Good:
<form onsubmit={(e) => {
  e.preventDefault()
  submit()
}}>
// or with modifier
<form onsubmit|preventDefault={submit}>
Code Bad:
<form onsubmit={submit}>
  <!-- Page reloads -->
</form>

Description: Use callback props for component events in Svelte 5
Code Good:
<!-- Child -->
<script>
  let { onclick } = $props()
</script>
<button onclick={onclick}>Click</button>
<!-- Parent -->
<Child onclick={() => console.log('clicked')} />
Code Bad:
<!-- Svelte 4 pattern -->
<script>
  import { createEventDispatcher } from 'svelte'
  const dispatch = createEventDispatcher()
</script>

Description: Add TypeScript types for event handler props
Do: Type callback props with proper event types
Code Good:
<script lang="ts">
  interface Props {
    onclick: (e: MouseEvent) => void
    onsubmit: (data: FormData) => Promise<void>
  }
</script>
Code Bad:
<script lang="ts">
  let { onclick }: { onclick: any } = $props()
</script>

Description: Effects with cleanup handle component lifecycle
Use untrack to prevent effect re-running
Pre-effects run before DOM updates
Don't: Try to read DOM in regular effect too early

Guideline: Use Svelte stores for shared state
Description: Stores provide reactive state accessible across components
Don't: Pass state through many prop levels
Code Bad:
// Prop drilling
<App {count} {setCount} />
  <Page {count} {setCount} />
    <Counter {count} {setCount} />

Description: Auto-subscribe to stores using $ prefix in components
Code Bad:
import { user } from './stores'
let userData
user.subscribe(u => userData = u)
// Must unsubscribe manually!

Description: Use derived for computed store values
Do: Use derived() for values based on other stores
Don't: Create separate stores and sync manually

Description: Readable stores wrap external data sources
Do: Use readable for subscriptions to external sources
Don't: Create writable stores for read-only data

Description: Slots allow flexible content injection
Do: Use slots for customizable content areas
Code Good:
Title

Content
Code Bad:
<!-- No slots -->
<Card title="Title" content="Content" />
// Limited flexibility

Description: Default content shows when slot is empty
Do: Add fallback content for optional slots
Code Good: No content provided
Code Bad:
<slot name="icon"></slot>
<!-- Nothing shows if not provided -->

Guideline: Use slot props for data passing
Description: Pass data from child to parent through slots
Do: Use let: directive to receive slot props
Don't: Access child data through bindings only
Code Good:
<!-- List.svelte -->
{#each items as item}
  <slot {item} />
{/each}
<!-- Usage -->
<List {items} let:item>
  <ItemCard {item} />
</List>
Code Bad:
<!-- No slot props -->
{#each items as item}
  <ItemCard {item} />
{/each}
// Less flexible

Description: Svelte styles are scoped to the component
Do: Write CSS in component style tags
Don't: Use global stylesheets for component styles
Code Good:
<style>
  .card {
    padding: 1rem;
    border-radius: 8px;
  }
</style>

Description: Global styles escape scoping and can cause conflicts
Code Good:
<style>
  .wrapper :global(.third-party-class) {
    color: red;
  }
</style>
Code Bad:
<style>
  :global(.card) {
    padding: 1rem;
  }
</style>
// Pollutes global scope

Guideline: Use CSS custom properties for theming
Description: CSS variables enable runtime theming
Do: Use custom properties for theme values
Code Good:
<style>
  .button {
    background: var(--primary, #007bff);
    color: var(--on-primary, white);
  }
</style>
Code Bad:
<style>
  .button {
    background: #007bff;
    color: white;
  }
</style>
// Hard to theme

Guideline: Use class: directive for conditional classes
Description: Class directive provides clean conditional class application
Code Good:
<div
  class="card"
  class:active={isActive}
  class:disabled={isDisabled}
>
Code Bad:
<div class="{`card ${isActive ? 'active' : ''} ${isDisabled ? 'disabled' : ''}`}">
// Messy string concatenation

Description: Svelte provides optimized transition functions
Code Good:
{#if visible}
Fades in/out
{/if}
Code Bad:
<div
  class="{visible ? 'opacity-100' : 'opacity-0'}"
  style="transition: opacity 0.3s"
>

Guideline: Use in: and out: for different animations
Description: Separate entry and exit transitions when needed
Do: Use in: and out: for asymmetric transitions
Don't: Use same transition for both directions
Code Good: Different enter/exit
Code Bad: Same both ways

Description: Use animate directive for list reordering
Do: Use animate:flip for list item movement

Guideline: Use local transitions for conditional blocks
Description: Local transitions only play when direct parent changes
Code Good: Only animates when immediate condition changes

Description: Actions add reusable behavior to DOM elements
Do: Use use: directive for DOM behaviors

Description: Actions should handle parameter updates and cleanup
Do: Return object with update and destroy

Description: If blocks conditionally render content
Don't: Use CSS display:none for conditional content
Code Bad: // Component still mounted

Description: Provide unique keys for list items to enable proper diffing
Do: Add unique key to each block
Don't: Use index as key or omit key

Description: Show fallback content when list is empty
Await blocks handle promise states declaratively
Code Bad:
<script>
  let user, loading = true, error
  fetchUser()
    .then(u => { user = u; loading = false })
    .catch(e => { error = e; loading = false })
</script>

Guideline: Use load functions for data fetching
Description: SvelteKit load functions run on server and client
Form actions handle server-side mutations securely
Don't: Create API routes for form submissions

Description: Properly handle and display errors from data loading
Layouts wrap pages with shared structure
Code Good:
// +layout.svelte
<Nav />
<main>
  <slot />
</main>
<Footer />
Code Bad:
// Every +page.svelte
<Nav />
<main>
  <!-- Page content -->
</main>
<Footer />

Description: Not all values need to be reactive state
Do: Use const for values that don't change
Code Good:
<script>
  const API_URL = '/api/users'
  const columns = ['name', 'email', 'role']
  let users = $state([])
</script>
Code Bad:
<script>
  let API_URL = $state('/api/users')
  let columns = $state(['name', 'email'])
  // Unnecessary reactivity overhead
</script>

Description: Key blocks destroy and recreate content when key changes
Do: Use {#key} when component must fully reset
Code Bad:
<UserProfile {userId} />
// Must manually reset internal state
<script>
  $effect(() => { resetState() })
</script>

Description: Each $ subscription adds overhead
Don't: Subscribe to same store multiple times
Code Good:
<script>
  import { user } from './stores'
  let userName = $derived($user?.name)
  let userEmail = $derived($user?.email)
</script>
Code Bad:
{$user?.name}

{$user?.email}

{$user?.role}

// Multiple subscriptions

Description: Code in onMount only runs in browser
Code Bad:
<script>
  const width = window.innerWidth
  // Error: window undefined on server
</script>

Description: Use TypeScript for type safety
Define interfaces for component props
Do: Create Props interface and type destructuring
Don't: Leave props untyped or use any
Code Good:
<script lang="ts">
  interface Props {
    name: string
    count?: number
    onUpdate: (value: number) => void
  }
  let { name, count = 0, onUpdate }: Props = $props()
</script>
Code Bad:
<script lang="ts">
  let { name, count, onUpdate }: any = $props()
</script>

Description: Proper HTML elements provide built-in accessibility
Do: Use button nav header main footer etc
Code Good:
<nav aria-label="Main">
  <button onclick={toggle}>Menu</button>
</nav>
<main>
  <article>...</article>
</main>
Code Bad:
Menu
...

Description: ARIA attributes enhance accessibility for dynamic content
Do: Add aria-* attributes for dynamic UI
Code Good:
<button
  aria-expanded={isOpen}
  aria-controls="menu"
  onclick={() => isOpen = !isOpen}
>
  Menu
</button>
<div id="menu" role="menu" aria-hidden={!isOpen}>
